ncat (1)            GNU Netcat            ncat (1)

NAME
       ncat - nc with networking options

DESCRIPTION
       ncat is the command-line version of the netcat utility,
       a powerful tool for transferring data between host
       systems, listening or connecting.


       ncat uses the Berkeley sockets programming interface.

       ncat has three main options: listen, connect, and
       background. All other options must precede these.

       The -h option displays a brief help message, and is
       used whenever something is unclear.


       The following options are available on every
       connection mode:

       -v, --verbose         Increase verbosity


       -i, --interactive     Start in interactive mode


       -c, --sh-exec <cmd>   Execute the command with sh


       -e, --exec <cmd>      Execute the command with default shell


       -x, --posix           Use POSIX compliance mode


       -w, --waitfor         Wait for a connection and close


       -l, --listen          Listen mode


       --listen=port         Listen port number (override


                                         default)


       --source=ip           Source ip address of the


                                         client socket (IPv4


                                         only)


       --source6=ip          Source IPv6 address of the


                                         client socket


       --source6-prefix=ip   Source IPv6 prefix of the client


                                         socket


       --source6-len=len     Source IPv6 prefix length of the


                                         client socket


       --source-port=port     Source port number of the client


                                         socket


       --remote=ip           Remote ip address of the client


                                         socket (IPv4 only)


       --remote6=ip          Remote IPv6 address of the client


                                         socket


       --remote6-prefix=ip   Remote IPv6 prefix of the client


                                         socket


       --remote6-len=len     Remote IPv6 prefix length of the client


                                         socket


       --remote-port=port     Remote port number of the client


                                         socket


       --ssl                 Enable SSL encryption


       --ssl-cert=cert       SSL certificates, in PEM
